title: Heat intolerance
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports shared decision documentation, thyroid-medicine interaction information, and specific thyroid-enlargement warning symptoms including shortness of breath, rapid growth, hoarse voice, and swallowing difficulty.
examination.rationale: The exact information and warning section supports clinician-assessed examination, systemic concern, entered plan, safety net, and follow-up without diagnosing hyperthyroidism.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports shared decision documentation, thyroid-medicine interaction information, and specific thyroid-enlargement warning symptoms including shortness of breath, rapid growth, hoarse voice, and swallowing difficulty.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.6 — information and thyroid-enlargement warning symptoms

investigations.text: Supports clinician suspicion context, mood or anxiety context, TSH, FT4, FT3, biotin history, repeat testing when symptoms change, and TPO antibody testing in recommendation-qualified cases.
investigations.rationale: The exact suspected-thyroid section supports clinician review of persistent symptoms, mood or anxiety, medicine, biotin, illness, and thyroid-test context without treating heat intolerance alone as diagnostic.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports clinician suspicion context, mood or anxiety context, TSH, FT4, FT3, biotin history, repeat testing when symptoms change, and TPO antibody testing in recommendation-qualified cases.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.3.2 — suspected dysfunction testing and antibodies

additions.text: Supports clinician suspicion context, mood or anxiety context, TSH, FT4, FT3, biotin history, repeat testing when symptoms change, and TPO antibody testing in recommendation-qualified cases.
additions.rationale: The exact suspected-thyroid section supports clinician review of persistent symptoms, mood or anxiety, medicine, biotin, illness, and thyroid-test context without treating heat intolerance alone as diagnostic.
additions.evidence_extract: Supports clinician suspicion context, mood or anxiety context, TSH, FT4, FT3, biotin history, repeat testing when symptoms change, and TPO antibody testing in recommendation-qualified cases.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.3.2 — suspected dysfunction testing and antibodies

source_limitations: Environment, measured temperature, sweating, weight, pulse, pregnancy, menopause, medicines, thyroid signs, test values, cause, diagnosis, treatment, referral, and urgency remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.

All previously supported GP legacy-item mappings were conservatively removed because exact item identity, direct support, and item-specific applicability were not independently established under the fail-closed contract.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
